DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO FEB-25-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CARMEN LAU'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, TO DEFENDANT FITNESS INTERNATIONAL, LLC, HEARING HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORY RESPONSES SETTING FORTH NAMES AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF INDIVIDUALS REPORTING ACCIDENTS IN THE SUBJECT SAUNA IS GRANTED. FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 8 AND 9 SHALL BE SERVED WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN TEN DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF A COPY OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. IT HAS LONG BEEN THE RULE NAMES AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF NON-PARTY WITNESSES WHO POTENTIALLY POSSESS RELEVANT FACTS ARE DISCOVERABLE. SEE: DAVIES V. SUPERIOR COURT, 36 CAL.3D 291 (1984); COLONIAL LIFE & ACC. INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT, 31 C3D 785 (1982); PUERTO V. SUPERIOR COURT, 158 CAL. APP.4TH 1242 (2008). THE COURT FINDS DEFENDANT'S DENIAL OF THE DISCOVERY WAS WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. HOWEVER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS MUST BE DENIED DUE TO FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P 2023.040 WHICH REQUIRES A REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS TO BE SET FORTH IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND DECLARATION FROM COUNSEL TO SUPPORT THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS ATTACHED TO THE MOTION. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-04-2020 Continued To Mar-08-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Fitness International, Llc Dba City Sports Club'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication (Stipulated Continuance From 3/30/20)
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. ALICE CHEN SMITH, Esq. of Yoka & Smith LLP (Tel: 213-427-2300) for plaintiff;
Atty. DAN AGUILAR, Esq. of Law Offices of Matthew Quinlan (Tel: 415-345-4282) for defendant.

Hearing Re: Defendant Fitness International, LLC Dba City Sports Club'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant Fitness International, LLC's motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication is denied in its entirety.

The doctrine of contractual assumption of the risk/release does not bar plaintiff's claims. There exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct was grossly negligent. There are also disputed issues of fact regarding (1) the existence of a dangerous condition on the property; and (2) defendant's actual and/or constructive notice of the dangerous condition.

A release does not apply to grossly negligent conduct. (Rosencrans v. Dover Images, Ltd. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1072, 1081.) "Gross negligence" is a "want of even scant care" or "an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct." (City of Santa Barbara v. Superior Court (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754; CACI 425.)

In this case, plaintiff creates a triable issue of fact regarding whether defendant acted with gross negligence. Whether defendant acted with gross negligence (and therefore whether the release is enforceable), generally is a question of fact for the jury to resolve. (See Jimenez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 546, 555; Chavez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 632, 640-642 [genuine issue of material fact as to whether fitness center was grossly negligent where evidence showed regular preventative maintenance was not performed]; see also Leon v. Family Fitness Center (No. 107), Inc. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1227, 1234 [reversing summary judgment for health club where collapse of sauna bench when properly utilized was not a known risk covered by release and assumption of risk provision]; Zipusch v. LA Workout, Inc. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1281, [reversing summary judgment for health club where there were triable issues of fact regarding whether health club negligently failed to inspect and maintain its exercise equipment].

Plaintiff alleges that she burned her left forearm when she fell from the second-row bench in the sauna into a "pit" or "hole" where the metal heater was located. Plaintiff avers that the design of the sauna and the lack of proper lighting caused the injury. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of a dangerous condition. There is disputed evidence regarding whether the lights were functioning in the sauna on the date of the incident, whether that alleged darkness contributed to the injury, and whether the hole and heater below the second-row bench created a dangerous situation. (See Singh Dec., par. 2; Lau Dec., par. 8; Wei Dec., par. 5; Walsh Dec., pars. 8 and 15-16.) There is also a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant had actual or constructive notice of the danger and whether defendant acted with gross negligence. Plaintiff presents evidence that the light was not functioning a week before the incident and defendant was notified of the issue. (See Wei Dec., pars. 8, 10-11.) Moreover, the instances of persons falling into the hole and onto or near the heater had been a recurring problem. (See Mathews Dec. [fell in hole in April 2018]; Tan Dec. [fell in hole in March 2017]; and Wong Dec. [fell in hole February 2014].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-30-2020 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-03-2020 Continued To Mar-30-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, To Defendant Fitness International, Llc
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. DANIEL R. AGUILAR, ESQ. of Law Offices of MAtthew J. Quinlan (Tel: 415-345-4282) appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. LINETTE J. YOON, ESQ. of Yoka & Smith, LLP (Tel: 213-427-2300) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Fitness International, LLC.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CARMEN LAU's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, To Defendant Fitness International, LLC.

Both sides stipulate to the pro tem judge. Hearing held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel further interrogatory responses setting forth names and contact information of individuals reporting accidents in the subject sauna is granted. Further verified responses to Special Interrogatories 8 and 9 shall be served without objection within ten days after service of a copy of this order with Notice of Entry. It has long been the rule names and contact information of non-party witnesses who potentially possess relevant facts are discoverable. See: Davies v. Superior Court, 36 Cal.3d 291 (1984); Colonial Life & Acc. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court, 31 C3d 785 (1982); Puerto v. Superior Court, 158 Cal. App.4th 1242 (2008). The court finds defendant's denial of the discovery was without substantial justification. However plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions must be denied due to failure to comply with C.C.P 2023.040 which requires a request for sanctions to be set forth in the notice of motion and declaration from counsel to support the amount of sanctions attached to the motion. Moving party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
